local_evidence.observed_shape: Gemini tmp chat files contain a top-level sessionId and per-message id fields.
local_evidence.example: session-2026-04-21T09-18-cc5794a8.json starts with sessionId and messages[0].id.

candidate.problem: parse_gemini_tmp_chat passed each message object directly into push_record, so extract_session_id selected the per-message id as session_id.
candidate.risk: Gemini prompt records were grouped by message ID instead of chat session, weakening repeated-prompt and session-level analysis.
candidate.decision: Preserve the top-level sessionId in the message metadata passed to push_record while leaving message timestamps and text extraction unchanged.

red.evidence: The new test failed with left message-id and right root-session-id.

green.evidence: The focused test passed after the parser injected top-level sessionId into per-message metadata.

full_verification.evidence: 10 UI helper tests passed, Vite build passed, 34 Rust library tests passed, 13 CLI tests passed, doc-tests passed, and strict clippy passed.
